title: rosa dumalis f. ovifera
intro: Rosa dumalis f. ovifera, commonly known as the wild rose hips, is the fruit of the Rosa dumalis plant. It is a type of rose species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The health benefits of Rosa dumalis f. ovifera include its high content of vitamin C, which can help boost the immune system and reduce the risk of scurvy. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions.

parts: Rosa dumalis f. ovifera has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, which contain flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, as well as essential oils like geraniol and linalool, known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of Rosa dumalis f. ovifera contain phenolic compounds, including gallic acid and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The stems and roots of the plant contain saponins, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. Additionally, the seeds of Rosa dumalis f. ovifera contain fatty acids and phytosterols, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular-protective effects.
preparations: Rosa dumalis f. ovifera has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. The tea made from Rosa dumalis f. ovifera is used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. Tinctures, on the other hand, are often used to relieve pain and reduce fever, as well as to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. side_effects: Rosa dumalis f. ovifera can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Excessive consumption of the plant's extracts or teas can lead to stomach pain, nausea, and diarrhea. Some individuals may also experience skin irritation, hives, or rashes due to the plant's allergenic properties. Furthermore, using Rosa dumalis f. ovifera in conjunction with blood thinners or diabetes medications can increase the risk of bleeding or hypoglycemia.
